stories.headline: SK Hynix Surges 13% in Record $26.5 Billion Nasdaq Debut as the AI Memory Boom Hits Public Markets
stories.key_takeaway: SK Hynix raised $26.5 billion and jumped 13% in its Nasdaq debut, the largest-ever U.S. listing by a foreign company, giving American investors direct access to the dominant supplier of the high-bandwidth memory powering the AI boom.

pull_quote.text: We've announced plans to double production capacity within five years, but every customer says, 'That's still not enough — we need more.'

stories.article_excerpt: SK Hynix priced its ADRs at $149, opened at $170 and closed up roughly 13%, raising $26.5 billion on Wall Street.

stories.headline: SambaNova Raises $1 Billion Series F Led by General Atlantic to Scale AI Inference Hardware
stories.key_takeaway: SambaNova raised $1 billion in a first close of its Series F at an $11 billion valuation, betting that specialized 'premium inference' hardware and marquee customers like JPMorganChase can carve share from Nvidia.

pull_quote.text: SambaNova's $11 billion valuation highlights the central role that fast inference now plays in the enterprise AI stack.

data_points.label: Claimed SN50 throughput vs Nvidia B200

stories.article_excerpt: SambaNova raised $1B at an $11B valuation to scale its inference chips as JPMorganChase signs on as a customer.

stories.headline: Together AI Raises $800 Million at an $8.3 Billion Valuation to Run Open-Source Models at Scale
stories.key_takeaway: Together AI's $800 million Series C at an $8.3 billion valuation, led by Aramco Ventures, is a bet that the future of AI compute belongs to cheaper open-source models on neocloud infrastructure rather than pricey closed APIs.

pull_quote.text: Intelligence is becoming a foundational resource for the modern economy, every bit as essential as electricity, bandwidth or capital. Our mission is to ensure that intelligence is abundant, not expensive.

stories.article_excerpt: Together AI raised $800M at an $8.3B valuation, betting cheap open-source models on neocloud infrastructure beat closed APIs.

stories.headline: Micron Lifts Its US Investment Target to $250 Billion to Feed the AI Memory Boom
stories.key_takeaway: Micron raised its US manufacturing commitment to more than $250 billion through 2035 and locked in five-year supply deals with hyperscalers and automakers, betting AI-driven memory demand will outrun supply for years.

pull_quote.text: As America celebrates its 250th anniversary, data and memory are foundational to the modern economy — and Micron is increasing our U.S. investments to more than $250 billion through 2035 to meet that moment.
pull_quote.role: Chairman, President and CEO, Micron Technology

stories.article_excerpt: Micron poured first concrete at its New York fab and raised its US spending plan past $250 billion, chasing AI memory demand.

stories.headline: Microsoft Launches a $2.5 Billion Initiative With 6,000 Experts to Get Enterprises Deploying AI
stories.key_takeaway: Microsoft is spending $2.5 billion to embed 6,000 experts inside enterprise customers, betting that AI deployment services, not models, are the new competitive battleground.

pull_quote.text: This goes beyond what has been labeled as Forward-Deployed Engineering, and will be the largest, most capable, outcome-driven engineering organization in the industry.

stories.article_excerpt: Microsoft is spending $2.5B on 6,000 experts to embed inside enterprises, betting deployment, not models, wins the AI war.

stories.headline: Apple's Revamped Siri Will Run on Google's Gemini, Court Filing Confirms
stories.key_takeaway: Apple's fall Siri overhaul will run on a custom 1.2-trillion-parameter Google Gemini model under a roughly $1-billion-a-year deal, cementing an unlikely alliance between two rivals and sidelining OpenAI.

pull_quote.text: We basically determined that Google's AI technology would provide the most capable foundation for Apple Foundation Models.

stories.article_excerpt: A court filing confirms the assistant Apple ships this fall will be powered by Google's Gemini, not OpenAI's ChatGPT.

stories.headline: Mistral's Next Open-Weight Model Enters July Early Access as Its Cost-Efficiency Bet Pays Off
stories.key_takeaway: Mistral is opening early access in July to a new 'fat but sparse' open-weight model, extending a cost-efficiency and sovereignty strategy already embodied by its self-hostable, benchmark-competitive Medium 3.5.

pull_quote.text: We have a very exciting model to come this summer — it will be open-weight, and we're opening early access to it in July.

stories.article_excerpt: Mistral confirms a new open-weight model for July early access, doubling down on a performance-per-cost strategy.

stories.headline: AgentPrizm Launches AgentMemory and AgentSkills to Give AI Agents Persistent Memory
stories.key_takeaway: AgentPrizm's new AgentMemory and AgentSkills platform bets that governance — audit receipts, validity windows and verifiable deletion — is what turns persistent agent memory from a feature into an enterprise trust layer.

pull_quote.text: Everyone is talking about agents that can act. But action without memory is shallow, and memory without governance is risky.

stories.article_excerpt: AgentPrizm's AgentMemory and AgentSkills launch bets that governed, provable memory is the missing layer for reliable agents.

stories.headline: TwelveLabs Raises $100 Million to Build Video-Understanding Foundation Models
stories.key_takeaway: TwelveLabs raised a $100M Series B co-led by NEA and NAVER Ventures to scale its Marengo and Pegasus video-understanding models, betting that querying existing footage is the under-served frontier of multimodal AI.

pull_quote.text: Five years ago, we made a contrarian bet: the substrate of machine intelligence is recorded reality in motion, not language. Language is downstream of understanding. Video is the data understanding has to answer to.

data_points.label: Share of world's data that is video

stories.article_excerpt: TwelveLabs raised $100M co-led by NEA and NAVER Ventures to make the world's video archives searchable and machine-readable.

stories.headline: China Unveils HAIRO, Its First Certified AI Hair-Transplant Robot, at WCHR 2026
stories.key_takeaway: HAIRO is China's first NMPA Class III-certified hair-transplant robot, an end-to-end AI system that signals the field's shift from surgeon-directed teleoperation toward regulated, higher-autonomy surgical automation.

pull_quote.text: The system's speed, stability and ability to operate with ultra-fine needles demonstrate its potential to become an effective clinical partner for experienced surgeons.

stories.article_excerpt: At WCHR 2026 in Seoul, Puncture Robotic debuted HAIRO, China's first NMPA Class III-certified AI hair-transplant robot.

stories.headline: DyTopo Rewires Multi-Agent LLMs on the Fly to Sharpen Collective Reasoning
stories.key_takeaway: DyTopo drops the fixed wiring of multi-agent LLM systems and rebuilds the agent-to-agent communication graph every reasoning round through semantic key-query matching, reporting an average 6.2-point gain over the strongest baseline.

pull_quote.text: Communication topology should be treated as an adaptive object conditioned on each round's goal rather than a static design choice.

data_points.label: Avg accuracy gain over strongest baseline
Forward pass per agent per round

stories.article_excerpt: A new arXiv paper lets LLM agents rewire who talks to whom at every reasoning step, reporting a 6.2-point average lift.

stories.headline: New Research Explains Why LLM Reasoning Still Fails at Long-Horizon Planning
stories.key_takeaway: A new paper argues that chain-of-thought reasoning behaves like a step-wise greedy policy that is provably too myopic for long-horizon planning, and shows that adding explicit lookahead lets a small model beat a much larger one.

pull_quote.text: Step-wise reasoning induces a form of step-wise greedy policy that is adequate for short horizons but fails in long-horizon planning, where early actions must account for delayed consequences.

stories.article_excerpt: A new paper argues reasoning ability and planning ability are not the same thing — and proves it.

stories.headline: Apple Sues OpenAI, Alleging a Systematic Theft of Hardware Trade Secrets
stories.key_takeaway: Apple's trade-secret suit against OpenAI turns a soured AI partnership into open litigation and makes hardware IP the newest battleground of the AI wars.

pull_quote.text: At every level, from members of its Technical Staff to its Chief Hardware Officer, and in coordination with business partners, OpenAI has been stealing Apple's trade secrets and confidential information.

stories.article_excerpt: Apple has sued OpenAI in federal court, alleging a coordinated theft of hardware trade secrets 'at every level.'

stories.headline: The Fed Taps Marc Andreessen to Co-Lead a Task Force on AI, Jobs, and Productivity
stories.key_takeaway: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh has embedded a prominent AI venture capitalist inside the central bank's monetary-policy machinery, betting that AI could reshape inflation and jobs even as critics warn about conflicts of interest.

pull_quote.text: Each task force will carefully consider whether policymakers' means and methods, analytical tools and policy approaches can be improved upon.

data_points.label: Co-chairs on Productivity and Jobs panel

stories.article_excerpt: Kevin Warsh has put a marquee AI investor inside the Fed's policy review, raising fresh conflict-of-interest questions.

stories.headline: The EU AI Act's Binding Rules Take Full Effect August 2 as the Enforcement Countdown Begins
stories.key_takeaway: On August 2, 2026 the EU AI Act reaches general application and the Commission gains the power to fine general-purpose AI model providers up to 3% of global turnover, turning a year of paper obligations into live enforcement.

pull_quote.text: There is no stop the clock, there is no grace period, there is no pause.
pull_quote.role: on rejecting calls to freeze the AI Act timeline

stories.article_excerpt: On August 2 the EU AI Act stops being a compliance memo and becomes an enforceable law with teeth for GPAI providers.
